page: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The memory configuration is constrained at 4.0 GB of RAM, representing a low-memory 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance includes 118 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core profile but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core Geekbench score is strong at 2108.0, and single-threaded gzip compression reaches 31.39 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is weak, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 2627.0 and a poor stress-ng multi-core rating. Memory bandwidth is also a bottleneck, with uncached read and write speeds rated as poor at 21.01 GB/sec and 14.28 GB/sec respectively. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2lds_v6 offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads requiring high single-thread clock speeds without massive memory footprints. The low memory-to-CPU ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or heavy multi-threaded applications. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, basic CI/CD tasks, and small-scale LLM inference text generation where the 4.0 GB memory limit is not exceeded.
description: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical Intel Xeon 8573C core operating at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 4.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low-memory 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and includes 118 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2108.0 and high single-threaded gzip compression speeds. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-focused, low-memory workloads. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic development or testing environments that do not require substantial memory resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v6 is a low-memory virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 118 GB local storage. Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, it offers strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2lds_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with 4.0 GB RAM and 118 GB local storage, featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A low-memory, compute-focused virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and integrated local storage for lightweight processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
2 dedicated vCPUs with 4.0 GB RAM and 118 GB local storage
Strong single-core performance and AVX-512/AMX support
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
